Narrative_Meaning: Kings dignify an older mature male, the patriarchy, business acumen, wisdom, knowledge and good advice.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Physician card is representative of medicine, healing and bad news. If a character is injured, or sick they can seek relief and respite through the Physician. If they aren't then the bad news could be simply that the Apple-a-day isn't actually medical insurance, but more likely there is a new Hitch on some character's horizons.

Significator.Plot: The Physician Plot is all about a medical situation, common to all medical dramas. It may be that the Plot centres on a disease, or plague that must be contained, and treated. Or the Plot may be about a specific Physician, perhaps new to a practice, or who has decidedly unorthodox practices.
Significator.Side: When a side is marked by The Physician they are marked as having a Skill, Talent, or Power that is essential to the well-being of one the other sides (usually the Dominant Side, sometimes the Above, Pressed or Below). Often the Physician will have to be persuaded, or paid, to use this ability, sometimes they will behave as an ally to anyone needs their help.
Significator.Character:
The Physician Significator indicates a Character who cares deeply about the well-being of others.

They Gain Yin when they rest, read or examine something or someone.

They Gain Yang when they ask questions, make observations, or write notes.

The Gain Chi when they solve a problem, heal a Wound, or reduce a Hitch.

They gain +1 Success Levels to all attempts to Heal Wounds or reduce Hitches.
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A compassionate and observant healer, dedicated to the preservation of life and the alleviation of suffering. They possess a keen eye for detail and a steady hand, finding their greatest purpose in mending the broken and restoring balance to those in their care.
Significator.Scene: The Physician Scene is when the Characters get better from any wounds they may have taken. This may consist of a Surgical Ordeal, or just a montage of push-ups. Yarn-Tellers can play this card to remove and Heal Wounds, and even some Hitches (such as a "Dying" Hitch - usually converting it to a Scars Hitch). It can even raise the dead, under the right circumstances. The Referee can (and should) limit the effectiveness of the card, requiring downtime, or new Hitches.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets one of the Character's Hitches. For example: In order to Hook a Arachnophobe Character, one should simply have to threaten them with a spider, or better yet, make the bad guy into a spider and have word of them reach the PC.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Hook just consists of terrible bad news, often medical in nature, such as an incurable (or at least perilous) disease diagnosis.
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: The aid of a brilliant and exceptional specialist—a savant whose extraordinary talents provide unique insights and peerless assistance.
Yarn.Fray_Description: This is a medical situation, maybe the PCs need Surgery (or to conduct it), perhaps they need to deal with a large scale accident, disaster, or plague.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The Fray is complicated by a question of medical ethics, there are four main forms, Autonomy (the right to refuse treatment), Beneficence (acting in the best interest of the patient), Non-maleficence (Do no harm), and Justice (insuring the most worthy get the necessary help). This may be as simple as rich client verses poor client or as complex as the cure being as bad as the disease.

NPC.Description: The Medic is a healer, a surgeon, or a doctor. They seek to treat and heal injuries and disease wherever they find it.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs get all the healing they can take, often plus some recovery time. This sort of intense medical attention can usually reduce, remove or resolve Quirk and even Flaw Hitches, it might reduce a Woe, but can rarely remove it.
Yarn.History_Description: The Physician indicates that the current situation has been caused by a disease, illness, plague or similar medical situation in the past. Perhaps the king has had a stroke, and is now bed-ridden, or the kingdom may have been decimated by a plague that has altered the balance of economic power in the country.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Physician indicates a situation where another requires aid, or intelligent assistance. This may be healing, diagnosis, treatment, care, nursing, mothering, or even tactical military support, but without this aid there will be probably be injury and death.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Physician indicates that the solution to this problem is fulfil a prescription, this might be by purchasing a medicine that a doctor has decided would be right for you, or by following a complex written plan or prophecy that was written before the situation arose, that describes the way out of the situation.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about a Character can help the Character, it might be someone who can patch your wounds, or fix your car. They may not be an Ally exactly, but they aren't an enemy either.
Revelations.Info_Description: The information revealed answers the how question. How are the enemy going to infiltrate the kingdom? How did the monster get in the house? How does one conduct open heart surgery? The answers can be exceptionally detailed or just the broad strokes though, and will usually tell you nothing about the reasons behind anything or even who is doing it. You may know the enemy is among the party guests, but not who they are. The smashed down door tells you how the monster got in, but nothing about what it looks like, or whether it left again.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Blood is usually a good indicator something bad has happened, but at times it can convey a lot more information to either a mage, a cleric, detective, physician, or a forensic biologist.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about the health of the Embodiment. This can be as simple as a splatter of blood revealing that someone (or something) was injured, a terrible cough, sweats or fever indicating some one is ill, or as complex as noticing that the locals all have mud-streaked but perfect, blemish-free skin, apart from the lord who is bathed and acne-ridden.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Cunning details are the careful touches of a design, secret compartments, doors, rooms and elevators, and of course the tales of actual cunning that people tell about a Character.

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked by something medical, it may be a scan or fitness test, or a contagious disease, or anaesthetic gas may block the way.

Tarot.Normal: +2 Success Levels to all attempts to treat Wounds on or by the Target while atop the pile.
Tarot.Crossed: +2 Failure Levels to all attempts to treat Heal Wounds on or by the Target while atop the pile.

Ordeal.Obstacles: 1 per Stage and 2 per Court Stage
Ordeal.Stage_Description: A short-cut, chance to skip ahead, or Lucky Break
Ordeal.Motional_Description: First survive the Stages, then worry about if anyone made it to the end before you.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Flowing forces may offer the Ebbing Side succour, in the form of medicines, Stabilised Wounds, and respite from the combat, if they simply surrender. This can have a profound psychological effect, adding an additional Ebbing Failure Level.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The Fight is a Duel, usually between two unbalanced individuals (one will be of higher power level than the other). Rules are formal, and normally observed at least by the more powerful or higher ranked opponent. Opponent may be a Monster.
  Facet    Monster  
Enigma ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Skulker — The Skulker is a shadowy figure often pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers, or magic, to cloud the minds of witnesses, then again perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational picking up the tab without realizing, or perhaps they are a Cryptid, an Alien Big Cat, Wolf man, Sasquatch or one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who maybe Fæ or the imaginary friend of one of the characters, possibly made real(ish) by an alien or an Increated.
Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. Skulkers may add Enigma Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they hide, and if they hide completely for a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Enigma Boon they may add Enigma Score cards to their Monster Pool (e.g. Enigma Boon 12 grants 1 card when they hide, but if the Skulker hides until Dawn / Dusk / Noon or Midnight they may add 4 cards to the Pool).
Skulkers must Discard or Play half their Monster Pool any time they are spotted or found out. Often this will be spent on an immediate Hide action. They may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any stealthy, or hidden, actions or preparations. Skulkers that are hidden may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation to another’s actions as though they were making them. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website Server, in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psych Defence in the mind of his puppet. A Sasquatch might Prepare a Flee action on the hunter they have spotted in their territory, that they will trigger with a Fear emotional effect.
Heresy ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Masker — The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they’re just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may add Heresy Score cards to their Monster Pool whenever they change or don their disguise (or mask) and Heresy Draw cards to the Monster Pool whenever their disguise works, or they are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced Success Levels to any Action to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time their disguise is seen, examined, or observed as part of the action.
Jeer ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Mockery — The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw) for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. The Mockery may add Jeer Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they are mistaken for what they mock. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn’t involve reminding someone of their own Mortality. If the Skeleton is mistaken for a living being, then it may add 1 card to its Mockery Monster Pool.
Liberty ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Nightmare — The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cause a Psych wound or appear in a dream. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Score (usually rolled) to everything they do. The Nightmare may spend at least one card from their Monster Pool to do something physically impossible, they might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, discard physical wounds, or appear in the waking world as examples. A Nightmare must discard a Monster Pool Card if they cross a threshold, barrier, or liminal space or time, or take a Psych Wound. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.
Nature ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Elemental — The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Reduced and +1 Score or Pips per Monster Pool card, to any and all actions to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend at least 1 card from its Monster Pool when soaking attacks that are not based upon its element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), are all example Elementals, as well as Forces-of-Nature and natural disasters such as storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).
Phoenix ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Paragon — The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion, such as Love, Forgiveness, Warmth, Satisfaction or Wonder. Paragon Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psych Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon begins with a Monster Pool of cards equal to the Phoenix Score. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool to multiply any Score by their Phoenix Score (usually rolled). The Paragon’s Monster Pool adds Phoenix Draw cards if the Paragon can find a source of their chosen emotion, and adds Phoenix Score cards if it can consume, interrupt or destroy this source (stopping a Character from feeling that emotion ever again would count as destroying the source, but so would eating them, changing their mood so they no longer feel that way woul be interrupting, as they could change back later). The Paragon must spend at least one card from its Monster Pool creating a Psych Attack for all observers whenever it is observed, which may be positive or Negative as the Monster / Yarn-Teller decides. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: Kings represent paths in the Labyrinth, and Cæster in the centre as well. A normal Bulmäs that tries to walk the Labyrinth would be lost, and would then be recruited into the Labyrinth. The Labyrinth can connect any point in space and time to any other, allowing Bulmäs to consciously time-travel through the Lea.  A King can allow a Bulmäs to create or reload a Chronolith or side-step depending on the power-level of the Bulmäs.

Stress.Description: Stress is dangerous, it can turn a simple mistake into a deadly danger, make a simple shock cause a heart attack, or make a Character hold a loaded gun to their own head.
Stress.Rules: All Hearts (♥) Suited Ordeal cards that target the Character for an Attack add the Character’s current Stress as Yarn so 13 Stress adds 66 Pips to each card, but then relieves Stress equal to the original Card Pips, plus all Strain and current Shock (although this new attack is a whole other ball game). Unsuited cards add current Stress as Chi (13 Pips), but remove only the current Stress Reduced (4 Stress), 1 Strain or 1 Shock.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can sometimes be medicine, surgery is carefully engineered trauma designed to lessen the damage of other Wounds, but such things can happen randomly. Sometimes a fresh wound will cure, or reveal to medics, a lingering problem allowing treatment to actually cure the underlying problem. It is worth remembering though that despite the narrative trope, a blow to the head will most likely cause a concussion, not cure amnesia.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma can affect a Hitch or lingering Wound, using Stress as Chi to directly heal Wounds, and reduce the Banes of Hitches.

Age.Description: An Age of Recovery is occurring. During this age a society heals and recovers from the wounds of the past, rifts will be mended, and the society will resettle into a new harmony. There may be a few scars and other signs of damage left behind, but most members have put the bad times behind them, whatever they were. During this age economic and cultural recovery will also occur, which may advance the society into a new age.
Age.Diegetic_Description: A time of healing and reconciliation, where society mends the divisions and scars of a previous era. It is a period of resettlement and the restoration of harmony, as the collective focus shifts toward rebuilding and the steady recovery of prosperity and culture.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Recovery a society will recover from whatever harm has previously befallen them. Typically this will mean a period of steady growth in membership, economic, artistic, technological and cultural successes are also possible to recover something previously lost.
